description: errorBoundary §19.6.4 nesting — an inner boundary catches its subtree's error BEFORE the outer boundary. loadItem("missing") fails inside the inner boundary; the inner fallback (eb-inner-fallback) shows. The outer boundary's OTHER child (eb-outer-child) survives and the outer fallback (eb-outer-fallback) is NOT triggered.
rationale: §19.6.4 / §19.6.6: nested boundaries follow inner-catches-first; an error caught by an inner boundary does NOT propagate to an outer boundary. So eb-inner-fallback shows, eb-outer-fallback does not, and the outer boundary's sibling content (eb-outer-child) is unaffected.
